method_atom: A strong-coupling observable of an otherwise intractable interacting system is computed by mapping it, through an exact duality between two equivalent descriptions, onto the problem of finding a single extremal (classical, saddle-point) configuration in a dual geometric space, whose boundary conditions are fixed by the external input data of the observable. The extremized action of that configuration yields the observable; the resulting divergences are tamed by an adapted regularization scheme, and the divergent structure is captured by a small number of control-parameter-dependent functions, with the finite remainder matched against an independently proposed closed-form expression.
object_atom: Planar gluon scattering amplitudes in N=4 super Yang-Mills gauge theory in the strong-coupling regime, with the gluon momenta as external inputs; the dual configuration is a classical string worldsheet in the curved (AdS) geometry whose boundary is determined by those momenta.
brief_reason: The duality-to-classical-saddle-with-fixed-boundary-data plus regularization-and-characteristic-functions machinery is transferable across fields, whereas the gluons, gauge theory, and string worldsheet are the domain-specific object.
